REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS LEGAL TITLE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS A PRELIMINARY MATTER, THE COURT IS INCLINED TO (BUT DOES NOT MAKE A DETERMINATION YET) TO FOLLOW MANANTAN V. NATIONAL CITY MORTGAGE 2011 WL 3267706. WHILE THIS AUTHORITY IS NOT BINDING, PLAINTIFF FAILED TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY AT ALL TO THE CONTRARY. HOWEVER, THE COMPLAINT, WHILE SUGGESTING THAT PLAINTIFF HAD ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE SALE, IS SO UNCERTAIN THAT IT IS NOT IMPOSSIBLE TO DETERMINE THE TIMING OF SUCH ACTUAL NOTICE. OPPOSITION MAKES IT EVEN MORE UNCERTAIN. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE A LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE FACTS (DATE, TIME, SOURCE OF INFORMATION, DATE OF ATTEMPT TO TENDER, ETC.) SURROUNDING THE ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 20 OF THE COMPLAINT. IF PLAINTIFF CAN IN GOOD FAITH, MINDFUL OF SHAM PLEADING DOCTRINE, ALLEGE LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE, PLAINTIFF SHALL ALSO ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING US BANK'S LIABILITY AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGED AGAINST US BANK, E.G. DUTY (CONTRACTUAL, STATUTORY, SUCCESSOR LIABILITY) TO PROVIDE NEW NOTICES AND/OR SERVE PREVIOUSLY RECORDED NOTICES, ETC. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CLEAR RECON CORP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - JOINDER. THE COURT WILL TREAT THIS MOTION AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF HER NEW CLAIMS BASED ON LACK OF BENEFICIAL INTEREST AT THE TIME OF THE FORECLOSURE. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING DEFENDANT'S COMPLIANCE WITH NOTICE REQUIREMENTS (RE: POSTING). PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE NOTICE HAS NOT BEEN POSTED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ONLY ADDRESS THE FACT THAT THE NOTICE POSTED ON THE GATE/FENCE SURROUNDING THE PROPERTY HAS NOT BEEN SEEN. TRIAL DATE OF FEBRUARY 10, 2020 IS VACATED. THE CASE IS RE-SET TO THE CASE MANAGEMENT CALENDAR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND NOTICE OF MOTION, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION THE COURT WILL TREAT THIS MOTION AS A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF HER NEW CLAIMS BASED ON LACK OF BENEFICIAL INTEREST AT THE TIME OF THE FORECLOSURE. THE COURT NOTES THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING DEFENDANT'S COMPLIANCE WITH NOTICE REQUIREMENTS (RE: POSTING). PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE THAT THE NOTICE HAS NOT BEEN POSTED. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENCE ONLY ADDRESS THE FACT THAT THE NOTICE POSTED ON THE GATE/FENCE SURROUNDING THE PROPERTY HAS NOT BEEN SEEN. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
The Court will treat this motion as a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. The motion is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of her new claims based on lack of beneficial interest at the time of the foreclosure. The Court notes that there are no triable issue of fact regarding Defendant's compliance with Notice requirements (re: posting). Plaintiff presents no evidence that the Notice has not been posted. Plaintiff's evidence only address the fact that the Notice posted on the gate/fence surrounding the property has not been seen. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - Joinder
Off Calendar Jan-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT CLEAR RECON CORP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication - Joinder. The Court will treat this motion as a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings. The motion is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of her new claims based on lack of beneficial interest at the time of the foreclosure. The Court notes that there are no triable issue of fact regarding Defendant's compliance with Notice requirements (re: posting). Plaintiff presents no evidence that the Notice has not been posted. Plaintiff's evidence only address the fact that the Notice posted on the gate/fence surrounding the property has not been seen. Trial date of February 10, 2020 is vacated. The case is re-set to the Case Management calendar. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Breanna Wood Fitzpatricck, 605.263.9638 of Mellen Law Firm for Plaintiff Teresita Pobre.
Jana Logan, 619.398.3354 of Kirby & McGuinn for Defendants Fay Servicing, LLC and US Bank.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS LEGAL TITLE DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Plaintiff has failed allege facts to support US Bank's liability as to any cause of action alleged against US Bank, e.g. duty (contractual, statutory, successor liability) .
Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-22-2019 Continued To Jun-19-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT U.S. BANK, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION AS LEGAL TITLE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. As a preliminary matter, the Court is inclined to (but does not make a determination yet) to follow Manantan v. National City Mortgage 2011 WL 3267706. While this authority is not binding, Plaintiff failed to cite any authority at all to the contrary. However, the Complaint, while suggesting that Plaintiff had actual notice of the sale, is so uncertain that it is not impossible to determine the timing of such actual notice. Opposition makes it even more uncertain. Plaintiff shall have a leave to amend to allege facts (date, time, source of information, date of attempt to tender, etc.) surrounding the allegations in paragraph 20 of the Complaint. If Plaintiff can in good faith, mindful of sham pleading doctrine, allege lack of actual notice, Plaintiff shall also allege facts supporting US Bank's liability as to each cause of action alleged against US Bank, e.g. duty (contractual, statutory, successor liability) to provide new notices and/or serve previously recorded notices, etc. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)
